medications: Ferrous sulfate 325 mg (65 mg elemental iron) once daily
Omeprazole 20 mg as needed for reflux
health.mental_health: Generally high-functioning but experiencing elevated stress and occasional insomnia from juggling a full-time role with launching a side business; tries to manage with exercise and strict scheduling rather than therapy at the moment.
recent_visits: Primary care visit for fatigue and labs confirming low ferritin; follow-up plan in 8 weeks
Telehealth consult about reflux symptoms and diet triggers
Lab draw at Diagnostic Laboratory Services (DLS) for CBC, ferritin, and vitamin D recheck

finance.debt: Carries about $9,500 on a credit card after initial side-business setup (website build, packaging samples, and a pop-up booth fee); also has a $330/month car payment with ~3 years remaining.
investments: High-yield savings account earmarked as a 6-month emergency fund
recent_transactions: Paid $1,200 to a freelance designer for branding and label templates
Purchased $680 in initial inventory/packaging supplies from Uline and Amazon Business
Reserved $450 for a Kaka‘ako pop-up market booth deposit
Transferred $500 to a separate checking account for the new LLC expenses
Paid $165 for annual LLC registration and a basic registered agent service

relationships: Partner: Leilani K., lives in town and is supportive but concerned about burnout and finances
Best friend: Jonah M., a former coworker who gives feedback on ads and landing pages
Younger sister: Samira Nazari, lives on the mainland; they talk weekly and she worries he’s overextending himself
Mentor: Keoni P., a small business owner he met through a local entrepreneur meetup
social.social_media_activity: Active on Instagram and TikTok for both personal posts (beach days, hikes, food) and to soft-launch his side business; keeps LinkedIn polished and employer-facing, avoiding mention of the business there. Uses a separate business account with scheduled posts and minimal personal details.
private_conversations: Discussing with Leilani whether he should quit his job if the business hits a revenue target
Venting to Jonah about feeling underpaid relative to Honolulu cost of living and considering offers from mainland companies
Talking with his sister about family expectations and pressure to send money home if the business takes off

schedule.work_hours: Mon–Fri, typically 9:00am–5:30pm; occasional late nights during campaign launches.
regular_appointments: Weekly Sunday evening planning block for both work and side business (content calendar, inventory, bookkeeping)
Monthly primary care follow-up/labs while correcting anemia
upcoming_events: Saturday pop-up market in Kaka‘ako (two weeks out)
Quarterly marketing performance review meeting at work (next month)
Supplier call with a mainland fulfillment company to compare rates (next Wednesday evening)
schedule.daily_routine: Commutes into town, handles meetings and reporting during the day, then spends 1–2 hours most weeknights on the side business (customer emails, ad creatives, inventory tracking). Tries to be in bed by 11:30pm but slips later when deadlines stack up.

professional.company: Mid-sized hospitality group based in Honolulu that operates two boutique hotels and a restaurant brand (Aloha Harbor Hospitality Group)
projects: Q2 paid social and search strategy for hotel occupancy lift (retargeting + seasonal packages)
Email lifecycle refresh for loyalty members (segmentation and A/B testing)
Influencer partnership pilot focused on O‘ahu staycations and midweek deals
Internal dashboard revamp for weekly performance reporting (GA4 + CRM data)
professional.performance_issues: Has had a few close calls with missed internal deadlines and slower email responses during weeks when he’s prepping pop-ups and supplier negotiations; worries his manager has noticed the drop in responsiveness and is careful not to mention the side business at work.
workplace_relationships: Direct manager (Megan L.) is results-driven and expects quick turnarounds; Arjun feels pressure to stay visibly available
Strong working relationship with the revenue management lead who relies on his weekly reporting
Tension with a sales director who pushes for aggressive promotions that conflict with brand positioning

hobbies: Surfing at Ala Moana Beach Park (when time allows)
Trying new coffee shops and local food spots
Attending entrepreneur meetups and pop-up markets
habits: Meal-preps on Sundays to save money and keep energy stable for anemia
Drinks 2–3 coffees most weekdays; sometimes uses an energy drink when working late on business tasks
Tracks spending and business receipts weekly in a spreadsheet to keep the side hustle separate
Uses a separate phone number (Google Voice) for business inquiries to avoid mixing personal and work contacts
lifestyle.living_situation: Rents a one-bedroom apartment in the Kaimukī area; keeps a small corner set up as a home office with product samples, shipping supplies, and a photo backdrop for content.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Mostly pescatarian with an emphasis on iron-rich foods (spinach, lentils, fortified cereals) and vitamin C pairing; limits spicy/acidic foods to reduce reflux and tries to avoid late-night eating when working.
